hero.eyebrow: Built for Infinite Dreams · Windows · Independent
hero.titleLead: The Dofus overlay that fits behind
hero.subtitle: {count} endgame dungeons, 800+ indexed monsters. Three-block cards readable between two turns. Offline, no memory reading.
hero.microCopy: Windows 10/11 · Works with Dofus 2.x and 3.x · Tauri-signed, VirusTotal-verified
hero.heroImageAlt: Dofus Companion open on Klime's Infinite Dreams strategy, three-block combat card: Unlock, Dangers, Tips

valueProps.sectionTitle: Three guarantees before your first run

items.title: No risk for your account
items.body: Doesn't read game memory. Doesn't simulate any input. Doesn't touch the Dofus client. Tauri-signed, VirusTotal-verified.

items.title: Readable in 10 seconds
items.body: Alt+D, window open in 150 ms. Three fixed blocks: what to unlock, what kills you, what helps. You scan, you close, you play.

items.title: Offline, no server
items.body: No tracker, no telemetry, no connection required. The overlay reads local JSON files, shows a window, that's it. This release is free to download and works offline.

items.title: Instant search
items.body: Type a few letters, arrow keys to navigate, Enter to open. 185 dungeons, 800+ monsters indexed.

items.title: Combat cards, 3 fixed blocks
items.body: Unlock, what kills you, what helps. Readable between two turns, sourced from Fandom + DofusDB, nothing invented.

items.title: Dedicated monsters view
items.body: Ctrl+M, type a name, its card opens. Designed to identify an Infinite Dreams add in 3 seconds.

items.title: Fine-grained settings
items.body: Opacity, density, parchment or dark theme, FR/EN language. Every toggle applies live.

items.title: Compact mode
items.body: For 13-inch screens or setups where Dofus already fills the window.

items.title: Keyboard-first
items.body: Nine shortcuts cover every action. Alt+D, Ctrl+M, Ctrl+L, and the full list inside settings.

steps.title: Download the installer
steps.body: 3.2 MB, Tauri-signed, VirusTotal-verified. Official download from dofuscompanion.com.

steps.title: Run the setup
steps.body: If Windows SmartScreen complains, that's normal, the Security page explains why. SHA256 hash shown for the paranoid (rightfully so).

steps.title: Alt+D in combat
steps.body: No account. No server ping. The app reads local JSON files, shows a window, that's it.

roadmap.title: Public, versioned
roadmap.subtitle: The most-requested items ship first. Everything is tracked.

columns.label: Shipped
columns.body: 4-block Combat Cards (141 bosses + 129 monsters), dedicated monster view Ctrl+M, search by monster name.

columns.label: Next
columns.body: Filter by mechanic archetype, enriched cards, broader coverage on level 200 Expeditions.

columns.label: Player requests
columns.body: Suggest, discuss. Popular requests move up, via the Feedback page.

support.title: We put the hours in so this exists.
support.body: Free to download, zero tracker. If the tool saves you runs, a coffee or a sponsor keeps the time we put in going.

items.name: Elhadi L.
items.body: Dofusian since 2010. Defined exactly what an endgame player wants to read mid-fight, and, more importantly, what they don't. Co-develops the overlay and the cards. The "four fixed blocks, not a Wikipedia page" logic comes straight from his Infinite Dreams nights.

items.name: Noureddine
items.body: Built the whole monorepo: landing page, Tauri overlay, bilingual scraper, update pipeline. Codes alongside Elhadi, features shipped when ready, not by quarter.

buildLines: no Ecaflip was betrayed in this build
this line changes, the app not so much

disclaimer.body: Dofus Companion is an unofficial application built by Noureddine & Elhadi L. Dofus® is a registered trademark of Ankama Games. This project is neither endorsed by nor affiliated with Ankama. No memory reading, no game automation: this is an information overlay only.

kerubim.firstUnlock: Well, well. A curious one. I was right to keep that rune.
kerubim.reUnlock: Yes, yes, I saw you the first time.

notFound.body: Maybe it existed, maybe it never did. In the meantime:
notFound.meta: If you expected an easter egg here, you lost. Or won.

download.smartScreenQuip: Windows looks at your executable the way a Sram looks at a tax collector: suspicious. Here's how to tell it we're here to help.
download.verifyMicroIntro: For the truly paranoid, and they're right to exist.

sha256.label: SHA256
sha256.pending: Will be published with the next release (SHA256SUMS.txt)

verify.title: Verify file integrity (PowerShell)
verify.body: Open PowerShell in the download folder and compare the hash:

previous.title: Other versions
previous.body: Every release and its assets are archived on the public releases channel.

smartScreen.title: If Windows shows a warning, here's why and what to do
smartScreen.body: The app is signed with the Tauri signature (for the auto-updater), but without a Windows EV certificate. Until the app has enough reputation-building downloads, SmartScreen may show "Windows protected your PC".
smartScreen.microCopy: You can verify the SHA256 hash before running, from the PowerShell block below.

virusTotal.label: VirusTotal
virusTotal.notSubmitted: The file has not been submitted to VirusTotal yet.

security.title: What comes in, what goes out, what stays on your disk.
security.subtitle: The app in 3 honest lists. Green, what it does. Red, what it doesn't. Yellow, what Windows will say anyway.

behavior.title: What the app does and doesn't

positive.heading: What it does
items: Reads local JSON files (the dungeon data).
Displays a window on top of Dofus.
Checks the releases channel for auto-update (HTTPS, Ed25519 signature).

negative.heading: What it doesn't
items: No memory reading of the Dofus process (Ankama ToS-compliant).
No keyboard or mouse input simulation (not a bot).
No telemetry, no analytics, no tracking.

fingerprints.title: SHA256 fingerprints
fingerprints.body: Asset SHA256 hashes will be published in an upcoming release (SHA256SUMS.txt attached). In the meantime, you can verify locally from PowerShell.

virusTotal.title: VirusTotal
virusTotal.body: Each asset is submitted to VirusTotal (70+ antivirus engines). The badge below shows the live result and links to the full report. An independent installer without an EV certificate may occasionally trigger a false positive on a heuristic (machine learning) engine, this does not affect binary integrity, and clears with project reputation or an EV certificate.

smartScreen.title: Windows SmartScreen
smartScreen.body: SmartScreen blocks executables without enough reputation. The app is Tauri-signed but lacks a Windows EV certificate. The warning will disappear with enough downloads or via an EV certificate.

items.label: Tauri signature
items.body: Each release is signed with the project's Ed25519 key, verified by the auto-updater.

items.label: SHA256 fingerprint
items.body: Hash published with each release, verifiable in PowerShell before running.

items.label: VirusTotal
items.body: Submitted on every release. Live badge on the Download page.

report.title: Report a vulnerability
report.body: For any sensitive security report, reach out via the Feedback page with "security" in the subject. Response within 72h, coordinated fix and disclosure.

changelog.title: Release history
changelog.subtitle: Every version. Clickable Git hashes. Nothing hidden, nothing glorified.
changelog.unavailable: Couldn't load releases, try again later.

meta.title: FAQ, Klime strategy, Infinite Dreams, ban risk
meta.description: Answers about Dofus Companion: Klime strategy for Infinite Dreams, ban risk, Windows install, privacy, uninstall. Independent overlay, no memory reading, 0 tracker.

faq.title: Frequently asked questions
faq.subtitle: Infinite Dreams, legal, installation, usage, data, feedback.

items.q: How do you beat Klime in Infinite Dreams?
items.a: Klime is a level 220 boss from Klime's Private Rooms, a recurring encounter in Infinite Dreams. Core mechanic: every character must end their turn on a white glyph, otherwise pacified state for 1 turn (or instant death in the Bravery expedition). To remove Klime's invulnerability, push an ally, a monster or a summon onto him. Wait until the start of turn 3 minimum before trying. Klime and the monsters generate a white glyph on their starting cell, lasting 2 turns. Tip: killing monsters before their turn delays the glyph mechanic. In the Daring expedition, position swap is limited to 10 times per turn per enemy. Full cards available inside Dofus Companion (Alt+D, type "Klime").

items.q: How does Dofus Companion help in Infinite Dreams?
items.a: Infinite Dreams drops you into random rooms with bosses and adds from different dungeons. You can't memorize all of it. Dofus Companion serves two purposes in Infinite Dreams: pull up a boss card (Klime, Sylargh, Missiz Frizz, Nileza, Count Harebourg, Merkator, Protozorreur, and more) between turns to re-read their strategy (Alt+D, type the name, arrows, Enter), or open the dedicated monsters view (Ctrl+M) to identify an unknown add in three keystrokes. 185 endgame dungeons covered, 800+ monsters indexed with weak element, HP and parent dungeon. The overlay stays above Dofus at all times, no Alt+Tab, no loss of focus.

items.q: Which other Infinite Dreams bosses are covered?
items.a: Every level 180+ boss that appears in Infinite Dreams has a card: Sylargh (Transporter), Missiz Frizz (Frostforge), Nileza (Laboratory), Count Harebourg (Count's Dungeon), Merkator (Aquadome), King Nidas (Palace), Queen of Thieves (Dark Court Throne), Protozorreur (Whale's Belly), Vortex (Eye), Chaloeil (Challenge), Captain Meno (Ship), Koutoulou Larva (Temple), and more. The full list is inside the app's search.

categories.title: Did Ankama sign off on this?

items.a: Dofus Companion respects Ankama's ToS: no process memory reading, no keyboard/mouse automation, no network packet reading. It's an information overlay, you search, you read, you play. Not affiliated with Ankama, not endorsed by Ankama.
The app doesn't interact with Dofus. No known ban cases linked to an information overlay. That said, Ankama is the sole judge, at your own risk.
A bot plays for you (macros, automation, memory reading). Dofus Companion displays strategies on top of the game window. You read, you apply them yourself.

categories.title: Setup, the 3 things that can go wrong

items.q: Windows SmartScreen blocks the setup. What now?
items.a: That's normal, the app doesn't have a Windows EV certificate yet. Click "More info" then "Run anyway". You can verify the SHA256 hash first, from the /download page.

items.q: My antivirus flags a false positive
items.a: Check the file's SHA256 on /download and compare with the VirusTotal report. If 0 detections on VirusTotal, it's a local false positive, report it to your antivirus vendor.
In %APPDATA%\Dofus Companion (Windows). Local JSON data only, nothing is sent to a server.
Check that no other app (Discord, AutoHotkey, OBS) is using the same shortcut. You can change the hotkey in the app's settings.

items.q: The overlay disappears in exclusive fullscreen
items.a: Dofus in exclusive fullscreen hides Windows overlays. Switch Dofus to "Borderless window" in the game's graphics settings.

items.q: How do I change the shortcut?
items.a: Right-click the app icon → Settings → Global shortcut. Any free combination works.
Nowhere. The app reads local JSON files and checks the releases channel for auto-update (HTTPS + Ed25519 signature). No telemetry, no analytics, no tracking.
Windows Control Panel → Apps → Dofus Companion → Uninstall. The NSIS installer removes the data folder. For MSI: same thing via Apps & features.

items.q: Are the strategies up to date?
items.a: Continuously updated with each release. Every sentence carries a provenance badge (official, community, LLM + citation). You can report an error via the Feedback page.

items.q: Will the project always be free?
items.a: On this release, the download and full usage are free. If we ever add advanced features that need dedicated infrastructure (multi-device sync, cloud storage, server-backed features), those additions could become paid. The base usage stays free.

items.q: How do I report a mistake in a strategy?
items.a: Open the Feedback page, type = bug or suggestion, with the boss name and what's incorrect. We read everything.

items.q: How do I translate or add a language?
items.a: EN is already covered. For another language, reach out via the Feedback page, we'll scope it together first.

feedback.title: Help us improve the Companion.
feedback.subtitle: A wrong strategy, a missing boss, a suggestion, a bug? Send a message. No account, no tracker, just a note that lands in our inbox.

form.subjectPlaceholder: Klime, white glyph in Bravery, for example
form.emailPlaceholder: So we can reply if needed
form.messagePlaceholder: Describe the bug, suggestion, or strategy to fix. The more specific, the more useful.
form.success: Message received, thanks. We'll get back to you if needed.

errors.generic: Failed to send, try again in a minute.
errors.rateLimited: You've sent multiple messages, wait a few minutes.
errors.validation: Check the fields, some are incomplete or invalid.

alternative.title: Prefer a direct channel?
alternative.body: For sensitive security topics, put "security" in the subject so we treat it as priority.

supportPage.title: Here's how to back the time we put in.
supportPage.subtitle: Dofus Companion is built on spare time by two people. If the tool saves you runs, here's how to support the project.

tiers.title: Buy us a coffee
tiers.body: One-time payment via Buy Me a Coffee, starting at 3 €. Fast, no account to create, no recurrence. The most direct way to say thanks.
tiers.qrLabel: Or scan the QR from your phone

transparency.title: Where does the support actually go?
transparency.body: Donations cover dev time and the technical costs of the project.

noPerks.title: What won't change
noPerks.body:
Boss and monster strategies, whether generated or improved in-house, stay accessible with no paywall.

On this release, a sponsor and a free user see the same app. A one-off donation funds the project, not a status.

alternatives.title: Can't donate? Do this instead.
items: Tell your guild about the app, the best marketing there is.
Report a bug via the Feedback page when you hit one.
Suggest a strategy fix via the Feedback page, that's valuable.
Keep us bookmarked on Buy Me a Coffee if you come back often.

sections.heading: What data do we collect?
sections.body:
THE LOCAL APP

The overlay installed on your machine doesn't collect, store, or send any personal data. Strategy JSON files are read only from your local disk. Your settings (language, opacity, shortcuts) are stored in %APPDATA%\Dofus Companion and never leave your machine.

THE WEB SITE

This site sets no cookies, embeds no tracker, runs no analytics. Standard hosting access logs from Vercel, IP, user-agent, path, HTTP status, are retained per Vercel's policy. We neither consult nor export them.

FEEDBACK FORM

If you use the Feedback page, the information you send (subject, message, and email if provided) reaches us by email via Resend. It is not stored in a database, not shared, and is used only to reply to you.

FINANCIAL SUPPORT (OPTIONAL)

If you choose to support the project via Buy Me a Coffee or GitHub Sponsors, those platforms handle the payment under their own privacy policies:

- Buy Me a Coffee, privacy policy: https://www.buymeacoffee.com/privacy-policy
- GitHub, privacy policy: https://docs.github.com/en/site-policy/privacy-policies/github-general-privacy-statement

We only receive the donation amount and, if you provide one, the display name you chose. No payment information (card, address) is shared with us.

sections.heading: Contact
sections.body:
For any privacy-related question, reach us via the Feedback page.

Under the GDPR, you have the right to access, rectify, or delete the data concerning you. Since the app collects nothing, those rights apply only to data handled by the donation platforms listed above (through their own channels).

Last updated: 2026-05-19

sections.heading: License
sections.body: The Dofus Companion application (Windows overlay) is proprietary software. Any unauthorized reproduction, modification, redistribution, or reverse-engineering is forbidden. Strategy data (combat cards, monster sheets), trademarks, logos, and graphical assets remain the property of the project. The source code of this website itself is available under the MIT license.

sections.heading: Warranty
sections.body: The software is provided "as is", without warranty of any kind. Use at your own risk.

sections.heading: Dofus trademark
sections.body: Dofus® is a registered trademark of Ankama Games. Dofus Companion is an unofficial application built by Noureddine & Elhadi L. This project is neither endorsed by nor affiliated with Ankama.

sections.heading: Changes to these terms
sections.body: These terms may evolve with the project. Any significant change will be announced in the site changelog, and the last-updated date below will be refreshed accordingly.

sections.heading: Governing law

meta.title: Discover the Companion: Klime strategy and Infinite Dreams
meta.description: Full tour of Dofus Companion. Concrete example: Klime strategy (Klime's Private Rooms, Infinite Dreams). Instant search, 3-block combat cards, dedicated monsters view, 185 endgame dungeons. Bilingual FR/EN, offline, no tracker.

hero.title: Your dungeon tome, opened on the right page.
hero.subtitle: Here's what the overlay lays over your screen. Every capture is the real app.

search.title: 185 dungeons under your fingertips.
search.body: Type the first letters of a dungeon or monster name. The overlay filters as you go, arrow keys navigate, Enter opens. No menu, no click, no dead time.
bullets: Alt+D opens and closes the overlay, even mid-fight

combatCards.title: Three blocks. No wall of text.
combatCards.body: The capture shows Klime's strategy (Klime's Private Rooms, a level 200+ boss you meet often in Infinite Dreams). UNLOCK says every character must end their turn on a white glyph and an ally has to be pushed onto Klime to remove his invulnerability. DANGERS covers the pacified state and the instant death in Bravery expedition. TIPS reminds you Klime generates a glyph at his starting cell. It all fits in a few lines, readable between two turns.
bullets: Content sourced from Fandom FR/EN + DofusDB, with verbatim citation when LLM extraction is used
Nothing invented: if a mechanic is missing, the block stays empty
Bilingual FR/EN with matching depth, handy when your mates play in English
combatCards.alt: Dofus Companion - Klime strategy for Infinite Dreams: Unlock, Dangers and Tips inside a combat card

monsters.title: Ctrl+M, know who's across the room.
monsters.body: This is the feature built for Infinite Dreams. Run into Harrogant, Cuirboule or Peunch in a wave? Ctrl+M, type the name, you get their card with weak element, HP, family and parent dungeon. Three seconds on the keyboard and you know who tackles, who pushes, who heals.
bullets: 800+ monsters with weak element and HP
Filter out lambda monsters with no notable mechanic, silence rule
One-click access to the parent dungeon from each card, useful when a monster from deep-endgame lands in your wave
monsters.alt: Dofus Companion - Infinite Dreams monsters view sorted by level, quick add identification

settings.title: The overlay adapts to your setup.
settings.body: Opacity to see Dofus behind, compact density for small screens, parchment or dark theme, FR/EN language, per-block content visibility. Every toggle applies instantly, no restart.
bullets: Comfortable / compact density to tighten vertical rhythm
Parchment (Dofus light) or dark (brown + gold) theme

density.title: For small screens.
density.body: When you're on a laptop or Dofus already fills the window, switch the overlay to compact mode: paddings halved, tighter spacing, same legibility. The toggle lives in Settings.

shortcuts.title: Built for a PC player.
shortcuts.body: Nine shortcuts cover everything. Alt+D to summon the overlay, Ctrl+M for the monsters view, V to toggle actionable vs detailed view, / to jump into the search. Full list inside the settings panel.
shortcuts.alt: Settings panel scrolled to the Shortcuts section

security.title: No risk for your account.
security.body: Dofus Companion is an information overlay. It doesn't read game memory, doesn't modify anything, doesn't simulate any input. Tauri Ed25519 signature, SHA256 hashes published with every release, VirusTotal submission on each build.
bullets: Never reads the Dofus client's memory
No automation, no macro, no bot
Runs offline, zero server, zero tracker
Ed25519 signature + SHA256 published with every release

cta.title: Download and get going.
cta.body: Windows 10 or 11. Under a minute to install, auto-update enabled. Free to download.
